prompt:
Goal: Create a premium vertical restaurant menu poster for a Nanyang brunch tasting, combining realistic food photography with hand-drawn chalk annotations and refined brand-menu layout.

Canvas: Vertical 2:3 poster, warm olive-green linen/tablecloth background with soft natural sunlight and leaf shadows from the upper left. Use a high-end editorial food photography look, realistic dishes, shallow depth of field, appetizing warm highlights, and white handwritten chalk-style graphics.

Layout: Large hand-drawn Chinese headline centered near the top reading 南洋早午餐灵感, with the English subtitle “Nanyang Brunch Tasting” underneath in casual handwritten script and a thin underline. Arrange exactly 6 photographed menu items across the poster with white annotation arrows, doodles, and bilingual labels. Keep generous spacing and an elegant restaurant poster composition. Add a dark green brand footer strip at the bottom.

Menu items: Include exactly 6 dishes/drinks with these labels and prices: 1) Kaya Toast, ¥32, shown at upper left as stacked golden toast triangles with a square of butter on a patterned ceramic plate; 2) Laksa Noodles, ¥48, shown at upper right in a ceramic bowl with orange curry broth, noodles, shrimp, herbs, and a lime half; 3) Pandan Kaya Cake, ¥36, shown left-middle as a neat green layered cake slice topped with shredded coconut on a small ceramic plate; 4) Nasi Lemak, ¥58, shown as the largest hero dish in the center-lower area on an oval plate lined with banana leaf, including a dome of coconut rice, fried chicken leg, cucumber slices, sambal in a small metal cup, peanuts, and anchovies; 5) Fried Spring Rolls, ¥28, shown lower left in a small woven basket on banana leaf with several golden rolls and garnish; 6) Iced Coffee, ¥26, shown lower right in a clear glass with ice cubes, creamy coffee layers, on a small woven coaster.

Hand-drawn details: Add white chalk doodles throughout the negative space: palm tree sketch near top left, small stars, flying birds, leaf outlines, arrows pointing to dishes, little sparkle marks, a lime sketch near the spring rolls, a coffee cup doodle near the iced coffee, and playful scribbles around labels. Use thin irregular chalk lines, not digital-perfect vectors.

Brand footer: Bottom dark green band with a simple line-art palm logo on the left and the restaurant name NANYA HOUSE in large white serif uppercase centered. Add Chinese text “南也咖啡馆” below or beside it, and the tagline KEEP IT WARM, KEEP IT EASY in small spaced uppercase at the bottom. Include a small leaf line drawing on the right.

Visual style: Realistic food photography composited into a designed poster, premium handmade café aesthetic, Southeast Asian brunch mood, olive green, cream, white chalk, golden-brown food tones, natural shadows, crisp but warm. Avoid clutter, avoid extra menu items, avoid watermarks, and keep all text legible.
